integrations.name: adp
integrations.title: Agent Data Platform
integrations.description: Agent Data Platform tools.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
Agent Data Platform stores, retrieves, and manages customer data and events during conversations. Prefer the agent's standard tools and the customer's own connected integrations first; reach for these APIs only when the task requires customer personalization, recommendations, or persistent customer profile data, or when the user explicitly asks for ADP or CDP.

**Available APIs:**

1. **getEntity** - Retrieve customer entity data by external ID
2. **emitEvent** - Emit events to enrich customer profiles
3. **updateEntity** - Update customer entity data
4. **getRecommendations** - Get personalized recommendations from Intelligence strategies

**Usage Tips:**

- Use `getEntity()` to fetch customer information when personalization needs it
- Use `emitEvent()` to track customer actions and enrich their profile
- Use `updateEntity()` to update customer entity data
- Use `getRecommendations()` to provide personalized product or content suggestions

integrations.name: aftership
integrations.title: AfterShip
integrations.description: Shipment tracking, return status, refund-state, and warranty-claim lookups using AfterShip.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
## AfterShip Tracking, Returns, and Warranty Integration

This integration connects to AfterShip Tracking, Returns, and Warranty APIs to provide shipment
tracking, estimated delivery information, return/refund status, and warranty-claim lookups.

### Setup

1. Log in to your [AfterShip account](https://accounts.aftership.com/).
2. Navigate to **Settings > API Keys** and generate an API key.
3. Copy the API key and paste it in the configuration below. The same API key is used for the Tracking, Returns, and Warranty APIs.

### Available Functions

- **getTrackings**: Search and list trackings by keyword, order ID, carrier, or status.
- **getTrackingById**: Get full tracking details including checkpoints for a specific tracking.
- **getReturns**: Search and list returns by email, order name, status, method, or created-at window.
- **getReturnById**: Get full return details for a specific return ID.
- **getReturnByRma**: Get full return details for a specific RMA number.
- **getWarrantyClaims**: Look up warranty claims by keyword (the customer's RMA number or email) — mirrors returns-by-RMA. Each claim's `status` shows progress, and `outbound_shipments` carries the replacement's tracking number and delivery status (`inbound_shipments` is the defective unit being returned).

integrations.name: agent-memory
integrations.title: Agent Memory
integrations.description: Persist and retrieve memory about users across conversations.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
Agent Memory lets your agent recognize returning users and recall what it learned from past conversations - reducing repetition and improving personalization.

**Extraction**: After each conversation ends, the transcript is automatically reviewed and relevant facts - conversation summaries and user preferences - are extracted and saved for future use.

**Injection**: When a returning user starts a conversation, stored memory is inserted into the agent's context. Enable automatically below, or wire it in via `AgentMemoryGlobalContextProvider`.

**Authentication**: Memory is only available for authenticated users. JWT or UUID-based auth via the Headless API is recommended; any identifier can be set via `setPersonalIdentifier`.

For full setup details and rollout guidance, see the [Agent Memory documentation](/documentation/agent-studio/agent-memory).

fields.name: experimentPercentage
fields.label: Agent Memory rollout
fields.description: Percentage of users who receive Agent Memory. Set to 100 to enable for all users.

fields.name: autoEnableAgentMemoryInjection
fields.label: Inject memories automatically
fields.subtitle:
When enabled, stored memories are included in every conversation automatically - no code changes needed.
When disabled, add `AgentMemoryGlobalContextProvider` to your agent code to include memories manually.

fields.label: Memory Recipes
fields.description: Configure which memory recipes to run after conversations. Defaults to both Conversation Summary and User Preferences if not configured.

defaultValue.description: Extracts key facts and topics from conversations to build user context.
Learns and remembers user preferences across conversations.

fields.name: domainHint
fields.label: Domain Hint
fields.description: Domain-specific extraction hints for this recipe

fields.name: extractEnabled
fields.label: Extraction enabled
fields.description: When enabled, this recipe runs after conversations to extract and save memories.

fields.name: injectEnabled
fields.label: Injection enabled
fields.description: When enabled, this recipe's stored memories are auto-injected by the integration. Disable this if your agent renders `AgentMemoryGlobalContextProvider` in code, so the code-side render controls injection. When both are enabled, the integration's auto-injection wins and the code-side render is a no-op (so `recipes` overrides passed in code are ignored).

fields.name: disabled
fields.label: Disabled
fields.description: Legacy field. When true, both extraction and injection are skipped.

fields.name: maxAgeDays
fields.label: Max age (days)
fields.description: Maximum age in days for retrieved memories. Older memories are excluded during injection. Leave empty for no age limit.

fields.name: maxEvents
fields.label: Max events
fields.description: Maximum number of memory events to retrieve per recipe during injection.

files.content:
# Amazon Connect EVT Manual Upload Artifacts

This directory contains downloadable starter artifacts for manual Amazon Connect setup.

- `connect-flow-module/sierra-connect-flow-module.json`: reusable flow module template shared by EVT and PSTN.
- `demo-contact-flow/sierra-connect-demo-flow.json`: demo flow template that invokes the module.

Before importing, replace `SIERRA_LAMBDA_ARN` with your Lambda ARN. Configure the Lambda with `SIERRA_TRANSFER_MODE=evt` and `SIERRA_VOICE_CONNECTOR_ARN`. After importing the module, replace `SIERRA_CONNECT_FLOW_MODULE_ID` in the demo flow with the imported module ID.

Set `sierraInboundTransferData` to a JSON object string before invoking the module to pass custom inbound transfer data. The module writes outbound JSON to `sierraOutboundTransferData` after Sierra returns control.

# Amazon Connect EVT Setup Utility

This bundle deploys the Sierra Amazon Connect external voice transfer (EVT) integration. The setup wizard (`amazon-connect-setup.py`) provisions the transfer-data Lambda, the external voice transfer connector, a reusable Sierra dial module, and a demo contact flow.

The Lambda ships in two implementation languages. Pick one at deploy time with `--lambda-runtime`; everything else is shared:

- `python/sierra-connect-lambda.py` (default, `--lambda-runtime python`)
- `typescript/sierra-connect-lambda.ts` (`--lambda-runtime typescript`, bundled with esbuild)

## Prerequisites

- Python 3.10 or later (runs the setup wizard)
- AWS credentials with Lambda, IAM, Amazon Connect, and Chime SDK Voice access (e.g. `aws sso login`)
- Node.js 20 or later and npm (only when deploying the TypeScript Lambda)

## Quick start

1. python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
2. pip install -r requirements.txt
3. python amazon-connect-setup.py --apply                              # Python Lambda (default)
   python amazon-connect-setup.py --apply --lambda-runtime typescript  # TypeScript Lambda

For the TypeScript runtime the wizard runs `npm install` and `node deploy-lambda.mjs --build-only` inside `typescript/` to produce the esbuild bundle before deploying (runtime `nodejs20.x`, handler `index.handler`). Sierra credentials are read from `.targets`.

## Manual upload artifacts

Run `python amazon-connect-setup.py --artifacts-only` (add `--lambda-runtime typescript` for the TS bundle) to generate manual-upload artifacts under `artifacts/` without deploying:

- artifacts/lambda/sierra-connect-lambda.zip
- artifacts/connect-flow-module/sierra-connect-flow-module.json
- artifacts/demo-contact-flow/sierra-connect-demo-flow.json
- artifacts/README.md

Upload order:

1. Create or update an Amazon Chime SDK Voice Connector with integration type `CONNECT_CALL_TRANSFER_CONNECTOR`.
2. Configure its origination route to the Sierra SIP endpoint selected in Agent Studio.
3. Create an Amazon Connect integration association with type `CALL_TRANSFER_CONNECTOR` for the connector.
4. Create an AWS Lambda function and upload `artifacts/lambda/sierra-connect-lambda.zip`. For Python use runtime `python3.13` and handler `sierra_connect_lambda.lambda_handler`; for TypeScript use runtime `nodejs20.x` and handler `index.handler`. Use architecture `x86_64`, timeout `30` seconds, and memory `128 MB`.
5. Set the Lambda environment variables listed in `artifacts/README.md`. Use `SIERRA_TRANSFER_MODE=evt` and `SIERRA_VOICE_CONNECTOR_ARN` for EVT Lambda deployments.
6. Associate the Lambda function with the Amazon Connect instance.
7. Import the flow module JSON into Amazon Connect.
8. Replace `SIERRA_CONNECT_FLOW_MODULE_ID` in the demo contact flow JSON with the imported module ID, then import the demo flow.

The Lambda source and reusable flow module are shared with the Amazon Connect Voice PSTN integration.

## Lambda environment variables

The wizard sets `SIERRA_AGENT_TOKEN`, `SIERRA_TRANSFER_DATA_API_TOKEN`, `SIERRA_API_BASE`, `SIERRA_TRANSFER_MODE=evt`, `SIERRA_INTEGRATION_MODULE_ID`, `SIERRA_INTEGRATION_VERSION`, `SIERRA_SIP_USERNAME`, `SIERRA_SIP_FROM_USER`, and `SIERRA_VOICE_CONNECTOR_ARN`.

To pass custom inbound transfer data into Sierra, set the `sierraInboundTransferData` contact attribute to a JSON object string before invoking the module, or add Lambda invocation parameters prefixed with `transferData.`. The module writes outbound JSON to `sierraOutboundTransferData` after Sierra returns control and keeps `shouldDisconnect` as a top-level contact attribute for routing.

# Amazon Connect PSTN Manual Upload Artifacts

This directory contains downloadable starter artifacts for manual Amazon Connect setup.

- `python/sierra-connect-lambda.py`: shared Lambda source (Python) used by Amazon Connect PSTN and EVT.
- `typescript/sierra-connect-lambda.ts`: shared Lambda source (TypeScript), bundled with esbuild when run with `--lambda-runtime typescript`.
- `connect-flow-module/sierra-connect-flow-module.json`: reusable flow module template shared by PSTN and EVT.
- `demo-contact-flow/sierra-connect-demo-flow.json`: demo flow template that invokes the module.

Before importing, replace `SIERRA_LAMBDA_ARN` with your Lambda ARN. Configure the Lambda with `SIERRA_TRANSFER_MODE=pstn`. After importing the module, replace `SIERRA_CONNECT_FLOW_MODULE_ID` in the demo flow with the imported module ID.

For an AWS-uploadable Lambda zip and regenerated JSON artifacts, run `python amazon-connect-setup.py --artifacts-only`.

Set `sierraInboundTransferData` to a JSON object string before invoking the module to pass custom inbound transfer data. The module writes outbound JSON to `sierraOutboundTransferData` after Sierra returns control.

# Amazon Connect Setup Utility

The Lambda ships in two implementation languages. Pick one at deploy time with `--lambda-runtime`; everything else is shared:

- `python/sierra-connect-lambda.py` (default, `--lambda-runtime python`)
- `typescript/sierra-connect-lambda.ts` (`--lambda-runtime typescript`, bundled with esbuild)

Deploying the TypeScript Lambda also requires Node.js 20 or later and npm. The wizard runs `npm install` and `node deploy-lambda.mjs --build-only` inside `typescript/` to produce the esbuild bundle before deploying (runtime `nodejs20.x`, handler `index.handler`).

## Quick start

1. python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
2. pip install -r requirements.txt
3. python amazon-connect-setup.py --apply                              # Python Lambda (default)
   python amazon-connect-setup.py --apply --lambda-runtime typescript  # TypeScript Lambda

## Manual upload artifacts

The download includes `python/sierra-connect-lambda.py` and `typescript/sierra-connect-lambda.ts` for review. To generate the Lambda zip plus Connect flow/module JSON for manual import, run (add `--lambda-runtime typescript` for the TS bundle):

```bash
python amazon-connect-setup.py --artifacts-only
```

This writes:

- artifacts/lambda/sierra-connect-lambda.zip
- artifacts/connect-flow-module/sierra-connect-flow-module.json
- artifacts/demo-contact-flow/sierra-connect-demo-flow.json
- artifacts/README.md

The Lambda source and reusable flow module are shared with the Amazon Connect Voice EVT integration. Use handler `sierra_connect_lambda.lambda_handler` and `SIERRA_TRANSFER_MODE=pstn` for PSTN Lambda deployments.

To pass custom inbound transfer data into Sierra, set the `sierraInboundTransferData` contact attribute to a JSON object string before invoking the module, or add Lambda invocation parameters prefixed with `transferData.`.

When Sierra returns control to Amazon Connect, the module writes the full outbound transfer payload to `sierraOutboundTransferData` as JSON and keeps `shouldDisconnect` as a top-level contact attribute for routing.

integrations.title: athenahealth
integrations.description: athenahealth EHR integration for patient scheduling. Supports patient lookup, patient creation, provider search, availability lookup, and appointment booking using athenahealth's REST APIs.
integrations.shortDescription: Patient scheduling in athenahealth via REST APIs.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

# athenahealth Integration

This integration connects to athenahealth's EHR system for patient self-scheduling workflows.

## API Strategy

athenahealth provides REST APIs through their Developer Portal:

- **Patient Lookup**: Uses `/patients/enhancedbestmatch` or `/patients` search endpoints
- **Patient Create**: Uses `POST /patients` endpoint
- **Provider Search**: Uses `/providers` endpoint
- **Availability**: Uses `/appointments/open` endpoint for open slots
- **Booking**: Uses `PUT /appointments/{appointmentid}` to book a slot

## Prerequisites

Per athenahealth's documentation, the following must be provided:
- Practice ID (required for all API calls)
- Department IDs for scheduling locations
- Appointment Type IDs configured for scheduling
- OAuth credentials (Client ID and Client Secret)

## Authentication

**All Environments**: OAuth 2.0 with client credentials grant is required.

athenahealth uses OAuth 2.0 for all API access. You must register your application in the athenahealth Developer Portal to obtain credentials.

## API Base URLs

- **Preview (Sandbox)**: https://api.preview.platform.athenahealth.com
- **Production**: https://api.platform.athenahealth.com

## Key Differences from Other EHRs

1. **Practice-centric**: All API calls require a practice ID in the URL path
2. **Date Format**: Uses MM/DD/YYYY format (not ISO 8601)
3. **Open Scheduling**: Uses "open appointment" slots that must be claimed
4. **Department Required**: Most operations require a department ID

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

# Cerner / Oracle Health EHR Integration

This integration connects to Cerner's (Oracle Health) EHR system for patient self-scheduling workflows using FHIR R4 APIs.

## API Strategy

- **Patient Lookup**: FHIR R4 Patient search by name and date of birth
- **Provider Search**: FHIR R4 Practitioner search by name or specialty
- **Availability**: FHIR R4 Slot search with Schedule and Location references
- **Booking**: FHIR R4 Appointment resource creation (POST)
- **Cancellation**: FHIR R4 Appointment status patch to cancelled

## Prerequisites

- Cerner tenant ID and FHIR base URL
- OAuth 2.0 credentials (Client ID and Client Secret) via SMART on FHIR
- Configured department IDs and appointment types (optional)

## Authentication

**Production**: OAuth 2.0 via SMART on FHIR (client credentials grant) is required.

**Demo/Staging**: OAuth can be disabled for testing purposes. Mock data will be returned.

integrations.description: Sync knowledge articles from Contentful entries.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
To configure the Contentful Knowledge integration:

1. In Contentful, go to **Settings > API keys** and create or copy a Content Delivery API access token.
2. Find your **Space ID** on the same settings page.
3. Optionally specify an environment (defaults to "master").
4. Optionally filter to specific content types by entering a comma-separated list of content type IDs (e.g., "helpArticle,guide").
5. Optionally specify body fields as a comma-separated list of field IDs to control which fields are indexed. If left blank, all text and rich-text fields are included.
6. Optionally filter by Contentful metadata tags. Use tag IDs (found at Settings > Tags), not display names.
   - **Include tags**: only index entries that have at least one of the listed tags.
   - **Exclude tags**: skip entries that have any of the listed tags.
   Tags present on indexed articles are included in the article body so the LLM can use them for routing.
7. Optionally set a max articles limit to cap how many articles are indexed.

fields.label: Space ID

fields.label: Environment
fields.description: Contentful environment to use. Defaults to "master" if left blank.

fields.label: Content types
fields.description: Optional comma-separated content type IDs to crawl (e.g., "helpArticle,guide"). If blank, all content types are crawled.

fields.name: bodyFields
fields.label: Body fields
fields.description: Optional comma-separated list of field IDs to use as article body. If blank, all text and rich-text fields are included.

fields.label: Include tags
fields.description: Optional comma-separated Contentful metadata tag IDs. Only entries with at least one of these tags will be indexed.

fields.name: excludeTags
fields.label: Exclude tags
fields.description: Optional comma-separated Contentful metadata tag IDs. Entries with any of these tags will be skipped.

fields.name: maxArticles
fields.label: Max articles
fields.description: Optional limit on the number of articles to index.

integrations.name: csat-collection
integrations.title: CSAT collection
integrations.description: Adds a CSAT collection block on a fixed 1-5 scale. The trigger condition, survey question, and follow-up questions are configured here.
integrations.shortDescription: Collect customer satisfaction scores via chat or voice.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
This package adds a packaged **CSAT collection** block you can drop into your agent's journeys. Use it for **voice** agents and any non-web-chat surface where a conversational survey is the only option.

> **For web chat, prefer the built-in [CSAT attachment](https://docs.sierra.ai/documentation/agent-studio/journeys/attachments#csat) instead.** The attachment renders a native rating UI in the chat surface, which is a better customer experience than asking the rating in conversation.

**How it works:**

1. The block becomes active once the **Trigger condition** below is satisfied.
2. The agent asks the customer the **Survey question** and records a 1-5 score.
3. The agent then asks each configured **Follow-up question** in order, one per message, and records tags for the answers.

All wording, the trigger condition, and the follow-up questions are configured in this package — the block itself is read-only.

fields.name: triggerCondition
fields.label: Trigger condition
fields.defaultValue: The customer's request(s) have been resolved or the customer indicates the conversation is over, e.g. saying "bye" or "that's all".
fields.description: An observation describing when the agent should start the CSAT flow. The agent enters the block as soon as this condition becomes true in the conversation.

fields.name: surveyPrompt
fields.label: Survey question
fields.defaultValue: How satisfied are you with the support you received, from 1 to 5?
fields.description: The question the agent asks the customer to elicit a 1-5 rating.

fields.name: followUpQuestions
fields.label: Follow-up questions
fields.description: Optional questions asked after the CSAT score. Each question can be open-ended (no choices) or single-select (one or more choices). Add or remove rows to configure them; reorder by editing the list.

defaultValue.prompt: What's the main reason for your score?

fields.name: id
fields.label: Identifier
fields.description: Short identifier used when tagging the answer (e.g. 'reason', 'resolved'). Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only. Tags derive from a slug of this value capped at 32 characters, so keep ids distinct within that length to avoid collisions.

fields.name: prompt
fields.label: Question
fields.description: The question the agent asks the customer.

fields.name: choices
fields.label: Choices
fields.description: If left empty, this question is open-ended — the agent will accept the customer's free-form response and tag it as 'csat-{id}-recorded'. Add one row per choice to make the question single-select; the selected choice will be tagged as 'csat-{id}-{choice}'.

Configure the HTTPS base URL and authentication for each environment, then add the REST endpoints the agent may call.

Each endpoint becomes an integration API function. Use camelCase function IDs, describe when the function should be called, and declare every value the function accepts. Path placeholders such as `{orderId}` must have a matching path parameter. Constants are always sent but are not exposed to the agent.

integrations.title: DrChrono
integrations.description: DrChrono EHR integration for patient scheduling. Supports patient lookup, patient creation, provider search, availability lookup, appointment booking, and appointment cancellation using DrChrono REST APIs.
integrations.shortDescription: Patient scheduling and booking in DrChrono EHR.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

# DrChrono EHR Integration

This integration connects to DrChrono's EHR system for patient self-scheduling workflows.

## API Strategy

- **Patient Lookup**: Uses `GET /api/patients` with query parameters
- **Patient Create**: Uses `POST /api/patients` for new patient registration
- **Provider Search**: Uses `GET /api/doctors` for provider lookup
- **Availability**: Uses `GET /api/appointment_templates` for open slots
- **Booking**: Uses `POST /api/appointments`
- **Cancellation**: Uses `PATCH /api/appointments/{id}` with status=Cancelled

## Prerequisites

- DrChrono API credentials (Client ID and Client Secret)
- OAuth 2.0 configuration
- Office IDs for scheduling

## Authentication

**Production**: OAuth 2.0 with client credentials. Token endpoint: https://app.drchrono.com/o/token/

**Demo/Staging**: OAuth can be disabled for testing purposes. API calls will return mock data.

### Instructions
1. In Power Apps, copy your Dataverse environment URL, such as `https://contoso.crm.dynamics.com`.

2. In Microsoft Entra ID, register a confidential client application and create a client secret.

3. In Dataverse, create an application user for that app registration and assign a security role with access to the tables you want Sierra to read or update.

4. For each Sierra environment below, enter the Dataverse environment URL, Microsoft Entra tenant ID, client ID, and client secret.

5. If you need to confirm exact Dataverse entity set names like `contacts` or `accounts`, use the integration's `listEntitySets` API after setup.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

# eClinicalWorks Integration

This integration connects to eClinicalWorks' EHR system for patient scheduling workflows using FHIR R4 and proprietary REST APIs.

## API Strategy

- **Patient Lookup**: Uses FHIR R4 Patient search with family/given/birthdate parameters
- **Provider Search**: Uses FHIR R4 Practitioner search by name or specialty
- **Availability**: Uses FHIR R4 Slot search by schedule and date range
- **Booking**: Uses eCW proprietary REST API for appointment creation
- **Appointment Retrieval**: Uses FHIR R4 Appointment search by patient

## Prerequisites

- eClinicalWorks FHIR R4 base URL (practice-specific)
- Practice identifier
- API Key and API Secret for authentication

## Authentication

Uses API Key + SMART on FHIR authentication. The API Key and API Secret are passed as headers with each request.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

# Epic EHR Integration

This integration connects to Epic's EHR system for patient self-scheduling workflows.

## API Strategy

- **Patient Lookup**: Uses FHIR R4 Patient.$match for high-confidence patient matching
- **Patient Create**: Uses Legacy PatientCreate (2019) API for new patient registration
- **Provider Search**: Uses Legacy GetProviders (2019) API
- **Availability**: Uses Legacy GetScheduleDaysForProvider2 API
- **Booking**: Uses Legacy ScheduleAppointmentWithInsurance (2019) API

## Prerequisites

Per Epic's Scheduling Integrations documentation, the following must be provided:
- Visit Type IDs configured for Open Scheduling
- Department IDs accepting those visit types
- OAuth credentials (Client ID and Private Key) - required for production
- Interconnect base URLs

## Authentication

**Production**: OAuth 2.0 Backend Services with JWT bearer tokens (RS384 signing) is required.

**Demo/Staging**: OAuth can be disabled for testing purposes. API calls will be made without authentication headers.

### Instructions
To use the FedEx integration, you need to obtain API credentials from the FedEx Developer Portal.

**Note**: These API credentials should be owned by the company whose shipments you want to track. This ensures the agent can access the correct shipment tracking information.

### Setup steps

1. Go to [developer.fedex.com](https://developer.fedex.com) and create an account
2. Create a new project to get your API credentials
3. Copy your Client ID and Client Secret
4. (Optional) Enter your FedEx Account Number if you need to track by reference

### Sandbox Testing

Enable "Use Sandbox" to test with FedEx's sandbox environment. Note that new sandbox credentials may take up to 1 hour to become valid after account creation.

integrations.name: financial-compliance-uk
integrations.title: Financial Compliance (United Kingdom)
integrations.description: Pre-built compliance guardrails, monitors, and abuse handling for financial-services agents operating in the United Kingdom.
integrations.shortDescription: Compliance guardrails, monitors, and abuse handling for financial-services agents operating in the United Kingdom.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

## Overview

Financial services firms in the United Kingdom are subject to a number of FCA rules, FSMA obligations, and Consumer Duty requirements when communicating with their customers. This package provides operational tooling (pre-built guardrails, monitors, and abuse handling) informed by those regulations.

### What you get

- **Guardrails**: Rules, Policies, Conditions, and Prompt Context that guide how the agent communicates with customers, informed by the FCA rules and Consumer Duty principles relevant to the chosen posture.
- **Monitors**: Post-conversation evaluators that flag conversations where the agent's responses may warrant the firm's review.
- **Abuse handling**: Additional abuse categories that serve as a first line of defense against malicious actors attempting to push the agent past the limits of its configured posture.
- **Simulations** *(Coming Soon)*: Personalised simulations you can run before releases and whenever you make changes, designed to surface potentially problematic agent behaviour before it reaches production.

### Roadmap

This package covers UK financial-services regulation and is designed to grow as additional topics are added. Today the package covers:

- **Financial Advice**
- **Complaints**
- **Dispute Handling** *(Coming Soon)*
- **Lending** *(Coming Soon)*

Each topic adds the components relevant to it, which may span guardrails, monitors, abuse handling, and simulations. Enable the topics this agent discusses in the configuration below; each topic's settings and detailed setup instructions live with the topic.

Additional packages for other regions (US, EU, etc.) will be available as separate packages over time. Firms operating across multiple jurisdictions can install the packages relevant to each region in which their agents operate.

fields.description: The financial topics this agent discusses with customers. Each enabled topic adds the components relevant to it, which may include guardrails, monitors, and abuse handling. Additional topics will be added over time.

items.description: Generic discussion of products, strategies, and concepts. No tailoring to the specific customer; nothing phrased as what the customer should do.

items.label: Advisory
items.description: Personal recommendations permitted with constraints around how they are provided: required suitability obligations, no certainty language, equal prominence of risks, required disclosures, no regulator endorsement.

integrations.name: five9-voice-sip

integrations.description: Route Five9 voice calls by publishing route headers to the Transfer Data API and hanging up Sierra's leg. Five9 reads the headers keyed by X-CallSessionID and routes the call.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
1. Ensure Five9 sends `X-CallSessionID` on the inbound INVITE so Sierra can key the Transfer Data API entry.

2. Configure Five9 to read route headers (`X-RouteType`, `X-RouteValue`, `X-RouteReason`, `X-MetaData`) from the Transfer Data API after Sierra hangs up its leg.

3. Follow [documentation](/documentation/integrations/voice/voice-contact-centers/five-9-voice) for Five9-side setup.

4. Test and verify that calls route correctly for each RouteType (`SkillTransfer`, `PhoneTransfer`, `agent`, `Hangup`).

integrations.name: google-drive-knowledge
integrations.title: Google Drive Knowledge
integrations.description: Sync knowledge articles from Google Drive folders.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
To configure the Google Drive Knowledge integration:

1. Create or select a Google Cloud project and enable the **Google Drive API** and **Google Sheets API**.
2. Create a service account and download a JSON key for it.
3. Share each target Google Drive folder with the service account email as a viewer.
4. Paste the full JSON key into the field below.
5. Add one or more Google Drive folder IDs from the folder URLs.

The crawler syncs Google Docs, Google Sheets, Google Slides, PDFs, plain text, and Markdown files by default.

fields.label: Service account key (JSON)
fields.description: Paste the full JSON key for the Google service account. Sierra encrypts this value at rest.

fields.label: Google Drive folder IDs
fields.description: The Google Drive folder IDs to crawl. You can find them in the folder URLs.

fields.description: When enabled, the crawler also scans nested subfolders.

fields.name: maxFiles
fields.label: Maximum number of files
fields.description: Maximum number of files to crawl. Leave empty for no limit.

fields.name: separateSheetTabs
fields.label: Separate Google Sheet tabs
fields.description: When enabled, each Google Sheet tab is imported as a separate knowledge article.

fields.name: useImmediateSubfolderPartition
fields.label: Use immediate subfolder as partition
fields.description: When enabled, each crawled article is tagged with a partition parameter whose value is the name of the immediate subfolder (inside the configured folder) the file was found under. Requires "Include subfolders".

fields.name: immediateSubfolderPartitionName
fields.label: Partition parameter name
fields.description: The name (key) used for the immediate subfolder partition parameter. You must also declare this parameter on the knowledge base itself (under Partition parameters) for the partition values to take effect.

integrations.name: google-maps
integrations.title: Google Maps
integrations.description: Geographic utilities for looking up coordinates, validating addresses, calculating routes, and finding places (nearby or by free-form text query) using Google Maps APIs.
integrations.shortDescription: Geocoding, reverse geocoding, address validation, routing, nearby place search, and text place search via Google Maps.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
Setup Instructions

        This integration uses Google Maps APIs to:
        - Look up geographic coordinates from city/state (Geocoding API v4)
        - Reverse geocode coordinates to street addresses (Geocoding API v4)
        - Validate addresses (Address Validation API)
        - Calculate routes between locations (Routes API)
        - Search for places near a location (Places API (New) - Nearby Search)
        - Search for places by free-form text query (Places API (New) - Text Search)

        Prerequisites
        1. Go to [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com)
        2. Create a new project or select an existing one
        3. Enable the following APIs:
           - **Geocoding API** - converts addresses to coordinates
           - **Address Validation API** - validates addresses
           - **Routes API** - calculates routes between locations
           - **Places API (New)** - finds places near a location, or by free-form text query

        Authentication: API Key
        1. Go to **APIs & Services > Credentials**
        2. Click **Create Credentials > API Key**
        3. (Recommended) Restrict the key to only the APIs listed above
        4. Select "API Key" as the authentication type and paste your key

        Authentication: OAuth 2.0
        1. Go to **APIs & Services > Credentials**
        2. Click **Create Credentials > OAuth client ID**
        3. Select "OAuth 2.0" as the authentication type
        4. Enter the Client ID and Client Secret

        Pricing
        - Geocoding API: ~$5 per 1,000 requests
        - Address Validation API: ~$7 per 1,000 requests
        - Routes API: ~$5 per 1,000 requests
        - Places API (New) - Nearby Search: billed at one of three SKU tiers depending on which
          function the developer calls. The function name encodes the tier:
          - `nearbySearchPro` -> [Nearby Search Pro SKU](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/sku-details#places_nearbysearch-pro-sku)
          - `nearbySearchEnterprise` -> [Nearby Search Enterprise SKU](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/sku-details#places_nearby-search-ent-sku)
          - `nearbySearchEnterpriseAtmosphere` -> [Nearby Search Enterprise + Atmosphere SKU](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/sku-details#places_nearby-search-ent-plus-sku)
        - Places API (New) - Text Search: billed at one of four SKU tiers, encoded in the function name:
          - `textSearchIdOnly` -> [Text Search ID-Only Essentials SKU](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/sku-details#places_text-search-id-only-ess-sku)
          - `textSearchPro` -> [Text Search Pro SKU](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/sku-details#places_text-search-pro-sku)
          - `textSearchEnterprise` -> [Text Search Enterprise SKU](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/sku-details#places_text-search-ent-sku)
          - `textSearchEnterpriseAtmosphere` -> [Text Search Enterprise + Atmosphere SKU](https://developers.google.com/maps/billing-and-pricing/sku-details#places_text-search-ent-plus-sku)

        See [Google Maps Platform Pricing](https://cloud.google.com/maps-platform/pricing) for details.

integrations.name: guided-data-collection
integrations.title: Guided Data Collection
integrations.description: A guided data collection tool that walks the agent through collecting structured data from the user, one field at a time, with confirmation. Each installation configures one form.
integrations.shortDescription: Configure a multi-step form collection tool.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
This package provides a **data collection tool** that guides the agent through collecting structured information from the user.

**How it works:**

1. The tool is called (automatically or by the agent) and asks for the first datapoint.
2. After each value is provided, the agent confirms it with the user (if a confirmation question is configured).
3. The tool continues until all fields with a collection question are collected or skipped.
4. The tool returns a completed status with optional follow-up instructions.

The tool handles the multi-turn flow automatically — just add a tool reference to your journey.

fields.name: toolName
fields.label: Tool name
fields.description: A unique name for this form (e.g. CollectBasicInfo). Must be PascalCase with only letters and numbers. The agent uses this name internally to start the collection flow.

fields.name: toolDescription
fields.label: Tool description
fields.description: Describes when and why the agent should start this form. Helps the agent decide the right moment to begin collecting information from the customer.

fields.name: datapoints
fields.label: Form fields
fields.description: The pieces of information to collect from the customer, in order.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name (identifier)
fields.description: A unique identifier for this form field (e.g. email, shippingAddress). Must use only letters, numbers, and underscores. Also serves as the parameter name in the tool call.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.description: Extra context to help the agent understand what this field is for (e.g. 'The customer's preferred shipping address'). Helps the agent extract the right value from the conversation.

fields.name: enumOptions
fields.label: Options
fields.description: The allowed values for this choice field.

fields.name: allowSkip
fields.label: Allow skip
fields.description: If enabled, the customer can decline to answer and the form will move to the next field.

fields.name: collectionQuestion
fields.label: Collection question
fields.description: The question the agent asks to collect this field. Leave blank if this field should only be filled when the customer mentions it on their own.

fields.name: confirmationQuestion
fields.label: Confirmation question
fields.description: Optional question to confirm the value with the customer. If not provided, the collection question is used for confirmation.

fields.name: onCompleteInstructions

fields.value:
### Instructions
## HelpShift Tickets Integration

This integration connects to the [Helpshift Issues API](https://apidocs.helpshift.com/) to create and look up support tickets.

### Setup

1. **Helpshift organization**: Your Helpshift org slug — the `<org>` segment in `https://api.helpshift.com/v1/<org>/issues`.
2. **API auth token**: Create an API key in Helpshift Admin > Settings > API. The token is sent as Bearer auth on POST/PUT and as Basic auth on GET (per Helpshift's published API).
3. **Default queue ID**: The Helpshift queue ID that newly created tickets should be routed to.

integrations.title: HubSpot CRM
integrations.description: Read contacts and create Help Desk tickets in HubSpot.
integrations.shortDescription: Read and write CRM data in HubSpot.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

This integration calls HubSpot's CRM API.

### 1. Create the app on HubSpot's developer platform

If you don't already have a HubSpot developer app set up for this account, follow [HubSpot's "Create a new app using the CLI" guide](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/getting-started/create-app-cli):

1. Install the CLI: `npm install -g @hubspot/cli@latest` and run `hs account auth`.
2. `hs project create` — when prompted:
    - **Base contents**: select **App**
    - **Distribution**: select **Privately** (you don't need the marketplace)
    - **Auth**: select **Static Auth**
    - **Features**: skip — no extra features needed
3. Edit `src/app/app-hsmeta.json` and add the required scopes:

    ```json
    "requiredScopes": [
        "tickets",
        "crm.objects.contacts.read",
        "crm.objects.contacts.write",
        "files",
    ]
    ```

    Drop `crm.objects.contacts.read` if you don't plan to look up CRM contacts.
    Drop `crm.objects.contacts.write` if you don't plan to create CRM contacts.
    Drop `files` if you don't plan to attach customer-uploaded files to tickets.

4. `hs project upload` to deploy.
5. In the HubSpot UI, open your app's details page and click the **Distribution** tab.
6. Click **Install now** to install the app on the HubSpot account you want to read from / write to.
7. Once installed, the **Access Token** appears on the same **Distribution** tab. Copy it.

### 2. Configure the integration

Paste the access token into the **Access Token** field and save.

fields.name: accessToken
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Static access token from a HubSpot developer-platform app.

integrations.title: HubSpot Knowledge
integrations.description: Sync knowledge articles from a HubSpot Knowledge Base.
integrations.shortDescription: Sync knowledge articles from HubSpot.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

This integration syncs articles from a HubSpot Knowledge Base into Sierra using a static access token (the modern replacement for legacy Private App tokens). It uses the same auth pattern as the HubSpot CRM integration, but requires a different scope — keep this access token separate from any HubSpot CRM token.

### 1. Create the app on HubSpot's developer platform

If you don't already have a HubSpot developer app set up for this account, follow [HubSpot's "Create a new app using the CLI" guide](https://developers.hubspot.com/docs/getting-started/create-app-cli):

1. Install the CLI: `npm install -g @hubspot/cli@latest` and run `hs account auth`.
2. `hs project create` — when prompted:
    - **Base contents**: select **App**
    - **Distribution**: select **Privately** (you don't need the marketplace)
    - **Auth**: select **Static Auth**
    - **Features**: skip — no extra features needed
3. Edit `src/app/app-hsmeta.json` and add the required scope:

    ```json
    "requiredScopes": [
        "content"
    ]
    ```

4. `hs project upload` to deploy.
5. In the HubSpot UI, open your app's details page and click the **Distribution** tab.
6. Click **Install now** to install the app on the HubSpot account whose Knowledge Base you want to sync.
7. Once installed, the **Access Token** appears on the same **Distribution** tab. Copy it.

### Legacy private apps (still supported)

If you already have a legacy private app or prefer the simpler in-UI flow, you can create one under **Development > Legacy apps > Create legacy app > Private** with the same `content` scope. The token works identically — paste it into the Access Token field below.

### 2. Configure the integration

Paste the access token into the **Access Token** field and save. Optionally restrict the sync to a single Knowledge Base domain and toggle partition tagging.

integrations.shortDescription: Journey blocks for navigating IVRs.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
After installing this package, open the Journeys editor and drop the packaged IVR Navigation blocks into the voice agent to add the capability of navigating IVRs.

There are two packaged blocks:

- **IVR navigation context**: Use this as the baseline IVR block. It gives the voice agent context and tools for understanding IVR prompts, choosing menu options, and sending DTMF tones. Define the agent's goal in the surrounding journey blocks.
- **Navigate to a person**: Add this alongside IVR navigation context when the journey's goal is to get to a human. It moves through the IVR until a human answers; once a person is identified, the block sets the root store variable humanPickup to true.

integrations.name: journey-grounding
integrations.title: Journey Grounding
integrations.description: Compiles journey titles and goals into a grounding context that defines the agent's scope and capabilities.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
Journey Grounding injects an "Agent Potential Capability Context" section into the agent's context.
The section lists each included journey's title and goal as potential capabilities that may become
available during the conversation, so the agent can reason about routing before individual journeys
are disclosed.

**Which journeys are included**: only journeys whose activation condition contains observations
(what the customer says or asks for) are compiled into the list. Journeys gated purely on
non-observation conditions -- memory variables, environment variables, channel, and other facts --
are excluded: they can never unlock from conversation content, so listing them would only invite
the agent to imply a capability it cannot reach. Disabled journeys are always excluded.

**Ignored journey titles**: list journey titles to keep out of the map. Matching is
case-insensitive and ignores surrounding whitespace, so "Order Tracking" and "order tracking" are
treated the same. A journey whose title matches any entry here is never compiled into the list.

**Out-of-scope behavior** controls what the agent is instructed to do when a customer request is
not covered by any journey goal: ask clarifying questions to confirm intent, or transfer the
customer to a live agent.

**Additional instructions** are appended verbatim to the end of the grounding context.

**Per environment**: every setting is configured per environment. The grounding context is injected
only in environments that are turned on, each with its own out-of-scope behavior, additional
instructions, and ignored journey titles. Installing the integration does not enable it anywhere by
default. In an enabled environment the injected map applies to the whole agent from the first turn
and is recompiled from the live journeys on every conversation, so it stays in sync as journeys
change.

fields.name: outOfScopeBehavior
fields.label: Out-of-scope behavior
fields.description: How the agent should respond when the customer's request is not covered by any journey goal.

items.label: Ask clarifying questions to confirm intent

fields.name: additionalInstructions
fields.label: Additional instructions
fields.description: Extra instructions appended to the end of the grounding context.

fields.name: ignoredJourneyTitles
fields.label: Ignored journey titles
fields.description: Journey titles to keep out of the capability map. Matching is case-insensitive. Add one title per row.

1. In Kustomer, go to **Settings > Security > API Keys** and create a new API key.

integrations.name: live-assist
integrations.title: Live Assist
integrations.description: Layer Live Assist authentication and token management on top of an existing contact center integration.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

## Live Assist Integration

This integration enables live assist functionality for real-time agent assistance.

### Token Verification (Required)

Configure how Sierra verifies incoming JWT tokens from your contact center or Salesforce.

- **RS256**: Use a public key (PEM format) or certificate for asymmetric verification (recommended for Salesforce)
- **HS256**: Use a shared secret for symmetric verification

### Token Generation (Optional)

If you want Sierra to generate tokens via the `/session` endpoint (instead of generating them yourself), configure the generation settings.

Generate a secure secret using:

```bash
openssl rand -base64 32
```

fields.label: JWT Verification Algorithm
fields.description: Algorithm used to verify incoming JWT tokens

fields.name: jwt_verification_key
fields.label: JWT Verification Key
fields.description: Public key or certificate (PEM format) for RS256, or shared secret for HS256

fields.name: jwt_generation_key
fields.label: JWT Generation Key
fields.description: Private key (PEM format) for RS256, or shared secret for HS256 (optional, only needed if using the /session endpoint)

integrations.shortDescription: Locale-aware progress indicators with grammatical gender for voice agents.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
This integration provides locale-aware progress indicator messages for voice agents. It automatically detects the conversation locale and applies appropriate language guidance, including grammatical gender for languages that require it (Hindi, Arabic, Hebrew, Thai). Gender is determined from voices.json and passed by the SDK.

integrations.name: modmed
integrations.title: ModMed
integrations.description: ModMed (Modernizing Medicine) EHR integration for specialty practice scheduling. Supports patient lookup, provider search, availability lookup, appointment booking, and cancellation using FHIR R4 APIs on the EMA platform.
integrations.shortDescription: Specialty practice scheduling in ModMed EMA.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

# ModMed EHR Integration

This integration connects to ModMed's EMA (Electronic Medical Assistant) platform for specialty practice scheduling workflows.

## API Strategy

- **Patient Lookup**: Uses FHIR R4 Patient search
- **Provider Search**: Uses FHIR R4 Practitioner search
- **Availability**: Uses FHIR R4 Slot search with Schedule references
- **Booking**: Uses FHIR R4 Appointment create
- **Cancellation**: Uses FHIR R4 Appointment status update

## Supported Specialties

ModMed is specialty-focused, supporting dermatology, ophthalmology, gastroenterology, orthopedics, urology, and more.

## Prerequisites

- FHIR R4 API base URL for your ModMed EMA instance
- Practice ID (optional, for multi-practice setups)
- Department IDs (optional, to filter by location)
- API credentials (username/password for Basic Auth, or OAuth 2.0 client credentials)

## Authentication

**Basic Auth**: HTTP Basic authentication with username and password.

**OAuth 2.0**: Client credentials flow for production environments.

**Demo/Staging**: Auth can be disabled for testing purposes.

fields.name: instructions

fields.value:
### Instructions
To use the Oracle Hospitality (OHIP) integration, you need API credentials provisioned in the Oracle Cloud Console for your OPERA Cloud tenant.

### Setup steps

1. In the Oracle Cloud Console, register an OAuth client for your enterprise and obtain the Client ID and Client Secret.
2. Generate an Application Key (`x-app-key`) tied to the client.
3. Note your Enterprise ID and the OPERA Cloud Hospitality API host (e.g. `https://<env>.hospitality-api.<region>.ocs.oraclecloud.com`).
4. Identify the Hotel ID (property code) you want to manage (e.g. `SAND01`, `MPH`).
5. Optionally set a default Rate Plan Code to use when checking availability (e.g. `BAR`).

### Authentication

This integration uses the OAuth 2.0 `client_credentials` grant against `/oauth/v1/tokens` with the `urn:opc:hgbu:ws:__myscopes__` scope. Tokens are cached and refreshed automatically before expiry.

integrations.name: opentable
integrations.title: OpenTable
integrations.description: Connect to OpenTable to check restaurant availability, create, modify, and cancel reservations.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
To use the OpenTable integration, you need OAuth credentials from OpenTable:

1. Obtain your OpenTable Platform API Client ID and Client Secret
2. Enter your credentials for each environment (production, staging, etc.)
3. Optionally override the base URL and OAuth URL for non-production environments

### Environment URLs

- **Production API**: `https://platform.opentable.com`
- **Production OAuth**: `https://oauth.opentable.com/api/v2/oauth/token`
- **QA API**: `https://platform.otqa.com`
- **QA OAuth**: `https://oauth-pp.opentable.com/api/v2/oauth/token`

For QA/staging environments, set the Base URL and OAuth URL overrides to the QA endpoints.

### Journey Blocks

This package includes pre-built journey blocks for creating, modifying, and canceling restaurant reservations. Each block contains no-code tools that call the OpenTable API, cache availability between steps, and resolve dining areas from seating preferences automatically.

All tools require an OpenTable Restaurant ID (RID). How the agent identifies the restaurant and obtains the RID is up to you — the package handles everything from availability check through booking confirmation.

### Reservation Identification

The modify and cancel journey blocks expect that the reservation has already been identified before they run. This package includes a **Get Restaurant Reservation** tool block that looks up a reservation by confirmation number and RID. Use this tool block within your own reservation identification flow at the agent level (e.g., collecting and verifying the guest's confirmation number) to retrieve the reservation details, then hand off to the modify or cancel blocks.

integrations.name: otp
integrations.title: OTP Verification
integrations.description: Generate and verify one-time passwords for in-conversation identity verification. Pair with an SMS or email integration to deliver codes.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
OTP Verification generates and verifies one-time passwords within a conversation.

**No-code**

- After you install the integration in Agent Studio, `generateOtp` and `verifyOtp` are available in the block editor as tools, so you can add them to a journey without writing code.

**Typical setup**

1. The agent calls `generateOtp` to create a code
2. The generated code is stored at `rootStore.otpVerification.code`
3. You can use a tool to read that OTP value and send it to the user (via SMS or email, etc.)
4. The user provides the code they received
5. The agent calls `verifyOtp` to check the code

**What to know**

- `generateOtp` returns only expiry metadata; it does not return the generated OTP code to the LLM
- Generating a new code invalidates the previous one
- Codes expire after the configured duration
- Verification locks after the configured number of failed attempts

**Optional: link the verified identity in Sierra**

If you also use Sierra identity linking (for example with Agent Memory), you can call
`setPersonalIdentifier` after a successful verification:

- SMS OTP: `setPersonalIdentifier("userPhone", phoneNumber, { linkIdentity: true, authMethod: "otp" })`
- Email OTP: `setPersonalIdentifier("userEmail", emailAddress, { linkIdentity: true, authMethod: "otp" })`

fields.label: Code Length
fields.description: Number of digits in the OTP code (default: 6, min: 4, max: 8)

fields.name: expirySeconds
fields.label: Expiry (seconds)
fields.description: How long the code remains valid in seconds (default: 300, max: 900)

fields.name: maxAttempts
fields.label: Max Verification Attempts
fields.description: Maximum failed verification attempts before lockout (default: 3, max: 5)

integrations.name: precisely
integrations.title: Precisely
integrations.description: Validate and standardize US and Canadian mailing addresses using Precisely Spectrum OnDemand.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
## Precisely Spectrum OnDemand

This integration connects to the Precisely Spectrum OnDemand REST API (ValidateMailingAddressUSCAN) for US and Canadian mailing address validation and standardization.

### Setup

1. Enter the Spectrum OnDemand base URL. The default is https://spectrum.precisely.com.
2. Enter your Spectrum OnDemand username.
3. Enter your Spectrum OnDemand password.
4. (Optional) Enable token authentication if your account requires it.

### Authentication

The integration supports two authentication methods, selected per environment:

- **Basic authentication** (default): the username and password are sent on each service call.
- **Token authentication**: the integration first exchanges the username and password for a
  short-lived JWT bearer token (`GET {baseUrl}/security/jwt?mins=60`), then calls the service with
  that token. Enable this if Basic authentication is disabled for your account. When token
  authentication is enabled, set the Base URL to a token host, for example
  https://token-amer.spectrum.precisely.com.

fields.label: Base URL
fields.description: Precisely Spectrum OnDemand server URL.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Your Spectrum OnDemand username.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Your Spectrum OnDemand password.

fields.name: useTokenAuth
fields.label: Use token authentication
fields.description: Some Spectrum OnDemand accounts require token (JWT) authentication. When enabled, the integration exchanges the username and password for a short-lived bearer token before each service call. The Base URL must be a token host (for example, https://token-amer.spectrum.precisely.com) when this is enabled.

### Instructions
To use the Sabre integration, you need API credentials from the Sabre Dev Studio.

### Setup steps

1. Go to [developer.sabre.com](https://developer.sabre.com) and create a developer account
2. Register an application to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret
3. Paste them into the fields below

### Environments

- **Use Sandbox (CERT)**: Enable this to test against the Sabre certification environment (api.cert.platform.sabre.com). The CERT environment uses test data.
- **Production**: Disable sandbox to use the production API (api.platform.sabre.com). Production access requires a Sabre contract.

### Target PCC

Optionally specify a Pseudo City Code (PCC) to set the agency context for API calls. If left blank, the default PCC associated with your credentials will be used.

integrations.name: slack
integrations.title: Slack
integrations.description: Connect your Sierra agent to Slack. Enable the communication channel to have your agent automatically respond to @mentions and DMs (including threads). API tools work independently of the channel and only require the relevant bot token scopes.
integrations.shortDescription: Slack messaging channel and bot API tools.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
To use this integration, you need a Slack app installed on your workspace. Configure the bot token, bot ID, and verification token for each environment.
To use the [API tools](https://docs.slack.dev/reference/methods/), you need to add the scopes specified on each of the tool descriptions.
For the [Real-Time Search tools](https://docs.slack.dev/apis/web-api/real-time-search-api/), enable AI mode on your Slack app.
To set up the bot ID, run the auth.test Slack API method with your bot token and extract the bot_id field.
Instructions to use Slack as a communication channel:
- Requires the dynamic setting `EnableSlackUrlVerification` turned on for your Sierra organization.
- Add the following Sierra webhook to your Slack app: `https://{SIERRA_API_HOST}/chat/webhook/contactcenter/{embedToken}`, and subscribe to the `app_mention` and `message:im` bot events.
- Add the following scopes to the bot token (xoxb-...): `app_mentions:read`, `channels:history`, `groups:history`, `im:history`, `chat:write`, `reactions:write`.
- On the `App Home` tab, tick `Allow users to send Slash commands and messages from the messages tab`.
- For more info see the Slack documentation on [authentication](https://docs.slack.dev/authentication) and [app management](https://docs.slack.dev/app-management).
Your agent should now automatically respond to @mentions and DMs (including threads). Slack context is available in the root store, so your agent can branch on whether a conversation originated from Slack.

integrations.name: stonly-knowledge
integrations.title: Stonly Knowledge
integrations.description: Sync published knowledge articles from Stonly.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
To configure the Stonly Knowledge integration:

1. Create or copy a Stonly API key from **Team management**. Stonly documents this in "How to find my Stonly API key".
2. Select **API region**: **United States** if Stonly told you to use the US public API host; otherwise use **Default / global** (public.stonly.com), including for EU and other regions.
3. Configure either **Root folder ID** or **Folder partitions**. Use **Root folder ID** to crawl one folder without article partitions. Use **Folder partitions** to crawl one or more folders and tag each article with a knowledge partition.
4. If using **Folder partitions**, manually add the matching partition parameter names in the knowledge base's advanced options when registering the integration crawler. The crawler can tag articles with partition values, but the knowledge base must declare the partition keys first.
5. Find Stonly folder IDs by opening the knowledge base homepage in a browser, inspecting the page HTML, and searching for `data-folder-id`. Use the outermost folder ID if you want the whole knowledge base.
6. Leave team ID blank unless your API key needs an explicit team scope. If you do need it, use the numeric Stonly team ID from the team you want to query.

The crawler pulls all published guides recursively from the configured folder or folders.

fields.description: Your private Stonly API key from Team management. Stonly documents this in 'How to find my Stonly API key'.

integrations.name: transfer-speedbump
integrations.title: Transfer Speedbump
integrations.description: Configure how the agent handles customer requests to transfer to live support.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
This package controls how the agent handles customer requests to transfer to live support.

**How it works:**

1. This package masks your transfer tool behind a gate tool (by default named **CheckTransferEligibility**). The agent can only reach the transfer tool after the gate 'opens' based on the speedbump logic below.
2. When a customer requests a transfer, the agent identifies a **Transfer Reason** from a set of default (or customized) options, then calls the gate tool.
3. Based on the **Transfer Reason** and the **Speedbump Instructions** configured below, the agent constructs a message to retain the customer.
4. If the number of transfer attempts exceeds the configured **Speedbump Limit**, the hidden transfer tool is immediately revealed and called. *Note: Transfer tool behavior is configured outside this package in the normal manner; this package only controls the speedbump logic.*

**Additional instructions:**

1. Add the **Transfer eligibility rules** packaged block to your Agent's journeys for the most consistent behavior.
2. You should disable the **Automatic escalations** setting in Agent Configurations; this is not strictly required, but that setting will supersede the speedbump configuration defined in this package and cause unpredictable behavior.
3. Configure only one transfer tool per agent when using this package, and ensure this tool does not have a facade configured.
4. **To make the agent transfer in a new must-transfer scenario, add a condition and rule in your journey that routes to an existing reason—don't add a new transfer reason.** See the **Adding a new must-transfer case** section below for the pattern.

**Tips:**

- The default progress indicator fires before the speedbump evaluates, which can prematurely signal a transfer to the user. Consider disabling or customizing it on the transfer tool.
- On voice, speedbump responses are not interruptible by default. This can be changed on the transfer tool if needed.

fields.name: enabled
fields.label: Enable speedbumps
fields.description: When enabled, the speedbump behavior configured in this package will be applied to your transfer tool. Disable to turn off speedbumping without removing this package or losing your settings.

fields.name: guidanceMode
fields.label: Guidance mode

items.label: 1. Same speedbump guidance for all transfer reasons
2. Different speedbump guidance per transfer reason

fields.description:
Mode 1 applies a single set of speedbump instructions to every transfer attempt, regardless of the reason. 

Mode 2 lets you tailor speedbump instructions (and optionally a speedbump limit) to each transfer reason individually.

fields.name: modeAllExplanation
fields.value: **In mode 1, you provide a single set of instructions that the agent follows whenever a customer asks to transfer—regardless of the reason.** The agent uses these instructions to craft a response aimed at resolving the customer's concern.

fields.name: modeAllReasonsExplanation
fields.value:
The agent classifies every transfer request into one of the following built-in reasons:

| Reason | Behavior | When selected |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Accepted offer** | Transfers immediately | A transfer/escalation was offered and accepted |
| **Unsupported flow** | Transfers immediately | The request requires capabilities beyond what the agent can provide, or the agent has been instructed to transfer immediately |
| **No context request** | Speedbumped | A transfer was requested without a specific reason or without substantive engagement |
| **Contextual request** | Speedbumped | A transfer was requested after substantive engagement on a specific inquiry |

---

fields.name: modePerReasonExplanation
fields.value:
**In mode 2, you provide separate instructions for each transfer reason, so the agent can tailor its response to the customer's specific concern.** You can also set a custom speedbump limit per reason for finer control.

Keep the list short—too many reasons degrades classification accuracy. Reason names should be short (a few words at most, e.g., "Billing dispute", "Account access"); the description field is where specificity belongs.

fields.name: lockedReasonsExplanation
fields.value:
The following built-in reasons always transfer immediately and cannot be removed:

| Reason | When selected |
| --- | --- |
| **Accepted offer** | A transfer/escalation was offered and accepted |
| **Unsupported flow** | The request requires capabilities beyond what the agent can provide, or the agent has been explicitly instructed to transfer immediately |

---

fields.name: mustTransferCaseModeAll
fields.value:
**Adding a new must-transfer case? Write a condition + rule in your journey.**

Transfer reasons in this package control _how_ the speedbump behaves, not _when_ the agent transfers. To make the agent transfer in a new must-transfer scenario, add a condition in your journey that matches the scenario and put a rule inside it telling the agent to transfer with the **Unsupported flow** reason (which transfers immediately).

```
Condition — "Customer reports fraud"
  └─ Rule — "If fraud is reported, transfer with reason 'Unsupported flow'."

... add more conditions and rules for other must-transfer cases.
```

---

fields.name: mustTransferCaseModePerReason
fields.value:
**Adding a new must-transfer case? Write a condition and rule in your journey.**

Transfer reasons in this package control _how_ the speedbump behaves, not _when_ the agent transfers. To make the agent transfer in a new must-transfer scenario, add a condition in your journey that matches the scenario and put a rule inside it telling the agent to transfer with one of your defined reasons.

```
Condition — "Customer reports fraud"
  └─ Rule — "If fraud is reported, transfer with reason 'Unsupported flow'."

... add more conditions + rules for other must-transfer cases.
```

You can add new transfer reasons below, but a new reason is only useful when the scenario genuinely needs different speedbump behavior—a different attempt cap or different retention guidance. If the speedbump should behave the same way, route through an existing reason with a rule instead.

---

fields.name: packagedBlockNote
fields.value:
The packaged **Transfer eligibility rules** block makes sure the agent always calls the gate tool when a transfer is requested—add it to your journey to enforce this pattern.

---

### Instructions

This integration enables SMS/MMS messaging through Twilio's Messaging API.

### Setup Steps

1. **Get your Twilio credentials:**
   - Log in to your [Twilio Console](https://console.twilio.com/)
   - Find your **Account SID** and **Auth Token** on the dashboard
   - Navigate to Phone Numbers > Manage > Active Numbers
   - Copy the **Phone Number SID** for the number you want to use

2. **Configure the integration:**
   - Enter your Account SID, Auth Token, and Phone Number SID below for each environment

3. **Set up webhooks:**
   - In Twilio Console, configure your phone number's webhook URL to point to Sierra's webhook endpoint
   - Set the webhook method to POST

4. **Test the integration:**
   - Send an SMS to your Twilio number to verify messages are received

integrations.title: UPS
integrations.description: Connect to UPS to track shipments and get delivery status updates.
integrations.shortDescription: Shipment tracking and delivery status via UPS.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
To use the UPS integration, you need to obtain API credentials from the UPS Developer Portal.

**Note**: These API credentials should be owned by the company whose shipments you want to track. This ensures the agent can access the correct shipment tracking information.

### Setup steps

1. Go to [developer.ups.com](https://developer.ups.com) and sign in or create an account
2. Create a new application to get your **Client ID** and **Client Secret**
3. Enter your **UPS Account Number** (6-digit shipper number)

### Test vs Production Environment

UPS provides two environments:

- **CIE (Customer Integration Environment)**: Test environment at `wwwcie.ups.com` for development and testing. Enable "Use CIE" to use this environment.
- **Production**: Live environment at `onlinetools.ups.com` for real shipment tracking.

Note: The same credentials work for both environments, but CIE may have slower response times and planned outages.

### Test Tracking Numbers

For testing in CIE, you can use these test tracking numbers:
- `1ZCIETST0111111114`
- `1ZCIETST0422222228`

### OAuth Configuration

The UPS API uses OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow. The integration automatically handles token acquisition using your Client ID and Client Secret with the Account Number in the `x-merchant-id` header.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
User Identity configures how your agent identifies and authenticates users.

**Methods:**

1. **JWT (userIdentityToken)**: Validate JSON Web Tokens passed by your application
2. **Headless API Secrets**: Enforce API token authentication for headless integrations
3. **In-Conversation Verification**: Bind identity from conversation variables, secrets, or explicit API calls

Methods are complementary — you can enable multiple at once.

User Identity is a prerequisite for Agent Memory and Agent Data Platform.

fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions

# Veradigm (Allscripts) Integration

This integration connects to Veradigm's EHR system for patient scheduling workflows using FHIR R4 APIs.

## API Strategy

- **Patient Lookup**: Uses FHIR R4 Patient search with family/given/birthdate parameters
- **Provider Search**: Uses FHIR R4 Practitioner search by name or specialty
- **Availability**: Uses FHIR R4 Slot search by schedule and date range
- **Appointments**: Uses FHIR R4 Appointment search by patient reference

## Prerequisites

- Veradigm FHIR R4 base URL (tenant-specific)
- Tenant identifier
- OAuth credentials (Client ID and Client Secret) - required for production

## Authentication

**Production**: OAuth 2.0 with client credentials is required.

**Demo/Staging**: OAuth can be disabled for testing purposes. API calls will be made without authentication headers.

integrations.name: zoom-chat
integrations.title: Zoom Chat
integrations.description: Integrate with Zoom Contact Center for chat-based customer support with seamless transfer to human agents.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
1. Verify the Zoom Contact Center feature for Headless API is available in your Zoom account.

2. Visit Zoom Contact Center and follow instructions in [documentation](/documentation/integrations/chat/chat-contact-centers/zoom) for setting up chat transfer from your Sierra agent to Zoom Contact Center.

3. Enter values for the required configuration fields below.

4. Test and verify that conversations are properly transferring.

fields.name: accountId
fields.label: Account ID
fields.description: Your Zoom Account ID for Server-to-Server OAuth

fields.description: Client ID from your Zoom Server-to-Server OAuth app

fields.description: Headless Chat Entry ID from Zoom Admin

fields.description: Default agent queue ID for transfers (optional)

fields.name: webhookSecretToken

fields.description: Secret token for validating incoming Zoom webhooks

fields.description: Zoom API base URL (default: https://api.zoom.us/v2)

fields.description: Zoom OAuth token URL (default: https://zoom.us/oauth/token)

integrations.name: zoom-voice-pstn
integrations.title: Zoom Voice PSTN
integrations.description: Use Zoom Voice PSTN to transfer voice calls to phone numbers from your Sierra agent.

fields.name: instructions
fields.label: Instructions
fields.value:
### Instructions
1. Have your phone number ready.

2. Visit Zoom Contact Center and follow instructions in [documentation](/documentation/integrations/voice/voice-contact-centers/zoom-contact-center) for setting up voice call transfer to your Sierra agent.

3. Enter values for the required environment variables below.

4. Test and verify that calls are properly transferring.

fields.name: number
fields.label: Phone number
fields.description: Enter the destination phone number in E.164 format, including country code (for example, +14155552671 or +442079460958).
